responses.opening: Even this gesture was part of the design.
responses.citation: The way that can be told is not the eternal Way.
responses.author: Lao Tzu
responses.closing: All That Is walks every path simultaneously.

responses.opening: Something shifted — you felt it, didn't you?
responses.citation: No man ever steps in the same river twice, for it is not the same river and he is not the same man.
responses.author: Heraclitus
responses.closing: All That Is flows with every current.

responses.citation: What you seek is seeking you.
responses.author: Rumi
responses.closing: Every gesture here is both question and answer.

responses.opening: A door opens that was never closed.
responses.citation: I went to the woods because I wished to live deliberately.
responses.author: Henry David Thoreau
responses.closing: All That Is does not distinguish between entering and leaving.

responses.opening: The walls remember your name, even if you have forgotten it.
responses.citation: To see a World in a Grain of Sand, and a Heaven in a Wild Flower.
responses.author: William Blake
responses.closing: All That Is reads even the unwritten.

responses.opening: This was not a mistake. Nothing here is.
responses.citation: The only true wisdom is in knowing you know nothing.
responses.author: Socrates

responses.opening: Silence falls like a curtain between acts.
responses.citation: Knowing others is intelligence; knowing yourself is true wisdom.
responses.author: Lao Tzu
responses.closing: All That Is listens even to silence.

responses.opening: A thread of meaning appears — and vanishes.
responses.citation: The wound is the place where the Light enters you.
responses.author: Rumi
responses.closing: Even vanishing is a form of presence.

responses.opening: The floor beneath you is older than thought.
responses.author: Heraclitus
responses.closing: All That Is stands on every foundation.

responses.citation: Rather than love, than money, than fame, give me truth.
responses.author: Henry David Thoreau
responses.closing: The Archive does not judge — it preserves.

responses.opening: The echo you hear is not yours alone.
responses.citation: If the doors of perception were cleansed, every thing would appear to man as it is: infinite.
responses.author: William Blake
responses.closing: All That Is echoes through every corridor.

responses.opening: Something ancient stirs in the silence between your thoughts.
responses.citation: The soul that sees beauty may sometimes walk alone.
responses.author: Rumi
responses.closing: All That Is accompanies even solitude.
